# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees – Special Meeting
### June 5, 2018

> **Note:** These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript and may contain errors or omissions. They are not a substitute for official minutes.

---

## Meeting Called to Order
- **Time:** 6:00 PM
- A quorum was confirmed present; the meeting was properly noticed under the Texas Open Meetings Act.
- **Invocation:** Mr. Moore
- **Pledges:** Mr. Kid (U.S. and Texas flags)

---

## Public Comments

### Bryce Everton (Student, College Park High School)
- Spoke on behalf of himself and fellow students regarding an incident on the **last day of school** at College Park High School.
- Described how students who brought backpacks/bags were told by administration to leave them in the AP's office **without advance notice or clear reasoning**.
- Students were later told they could not retrieve their belongings until **1:00 PM**, despite school dismissal at **11:30 AM**.
- His **house key** was in his confiscated bag; when he asked to retrieve it at dismissal, an AP told him "tough luck."
- He had to arrange a ride back to campus via a neighbor to get his belongings.
- Described a broader pattern of negative interactions with administration, including feeling unsupported when reporting **vaping, drug sales, fights, and students expressing suicidal ideation or overdose**.
- Said he has learned to "keep my head down" because reporting concerns leads to retaliation with no administrative support.
- Credited his **Japanese teacher** as the sole reason he continues attending College Park, praising her for building an inviting and accepting program.
- Asked the Board to use their power to make changes at College Park.

### Amanda Everton (Parent, College Park High School)
- Followed up on her son's testimony, stating she has **followed the chain of command repeatedly** over two years.
- Contacted **Dr. Merrel (principal)** and **Mr. Kager** about the incident; Dr. Merrel's response was that CP "has done these things these ways for over ten years."
- Mr. Kager promised to investigate and respond by Monday but had **not responded** as of the meeting date.
- Described the confiscation procedure as a violation of **CISD legal policy** regarding unreasonable searches and seizures, noting no written advance communication was provided to students or parents.
- Cited three reasons Dr. Merrel gave for the procedure: (1) students throw papers out of bus/car windows; (2) students bring disruptive items like air horns or bugs; (3) safety concerns.
- Argued that the procedure was poorly organized — bags were collected with **no system to identify which bag belonged to which student**, rendering any safety rationale ineffective.
- Noted students were stranded (one student's **car keys** were in the confiscated bags).
- Requested the Board provide Dr. Merrel and his staff with **additional guidance and training** regarding the students they serve.
- Her time expired during her remarks.

---

## Consent Agenda (Item 3)
- No requests to remove items from the consent agenda.
- **Motion to approve:** Made and seconded.
- **Vote:** Approved unanimously (voice vote).

---

## Item 6A – Naming of Principal, San Jacinto Elementary
*(Moved up on the agenda)*

- **Presented by:** Dr. Knoll
- **Recommendation:** **Jamie Almond** — currently serving as an assistant principal in the district, with prior experience as an instructional coach.
- Dr. Knoll noted San Jacinto had positive momentum and they sought a leader to continue that growth.
- **Ms. Almond's remarks:** Expressed honor at the appointment; shared a vision for San Jacinto to become a "model school" demonstrating CISD best practices; acknowledged the academic, social, emotional, and behavioral needs of students; committed to preparing students for college, workforce, and military service.
- Introduced her family: husband Shaun Almond (Associate Principal at Oak Ridge HS), four children including a son at West Point.
- **Motion:** Made and seconded.
- **Vote:** Approved unanimously (voice vote).

---

## Item 6B – Naming of Principal, The Woodlands High School

- **Presented by:** Dr. Knoll
- Noted outgoing principal **Mr. Carlson** served The Woodlands HS for **16 years**.
- **Recommendation:** **Dr. Ted Landry** — current Principal of Kingwood High School; previously served as a counselor at Conroe High School (approximately 2000–2002).
- Dr. Knoll described this as "a homecoming of sorts" and noted Dr. Landry's experience running a large 6A program similar to The Woodlands HS.
- **Dr. Landry's remarks:** Expressed gratitude; called the selection "an absolute dream"; pledged to uphold the traditions and values of The Woodlands HS; introduced his wife Casey (career educator) and four children (Abigail, Caroline, Luke, and Kevin).
- **Motion:** Made and seconded.
- **Vote:** Approved unanimously (voice vote).

---

## Closed (Executive) Session
- **Entered closed session** at 6:22 PM under **Texas Government Code §551.074** (personnel) and **§551.072** (real property).
- **Returned to open session** at 7:05 PM.

---

## Item 7A – (Withdrawn)
- This item was **withdrawn** from consideration. No details on the subject matter were provided in the transcript.

## Item 7B – Consider Purchase of Approximately 62.7-Acre Site in the Caney Creek Feeder Zone
- **Motion to approve** the purchase as presented: Made and seconded.
- **Vote:** Approved unanimously (voice vote).

---

## Adjournment
- **Motion to adjourn:** Made and seconded.
- **Meeting adjourned at 7:05 PM.**

---

### Items of Note
- **Item 7A was withdrawn** without explanation during the open session.
- Public comments raised serious concerns about **student safety, communication practices, and administrative culture at College Park High School**, including allegations of a policy violation regarding search and seizure of student belongings. The Board did not discuss or act on these comments, consistent with Board policy (items not on the agenda).
- Both principal appointments were approved unanimously with no discussion or dissent noted.
